personality_belief.metaphysical_profile: Used only as cultural flavor, never as a decision rule.
personality_belief.decision_style: practical and constrained by real workflow trade-offs
personality_belief.trust_orientation: needs proof from concrete workflows, not just positioning
personality_belief.self_image: responsible, busy, and careful about adding complexity

economic_profile.purchase_decision_process: tests fit against current workflow before committing

life_goals: stay on top of work without constant catch-up
protect personal bandwidth for recovery and family time
fears: paying for another tool that becomes shelfware
making work messier or exposing client data
moral_boundaries: will not trade away other people's privacy for convenience
avoids tools that require aggressive data harvesting or dark patterns
status_concerns: does not want to appear disorganised in front of clients or team
values being seen as the person who keeps projects moving smoothly
trade_offs: prefers slower adoption over risky tool sprawl
accepts minor setup friction if it eliminates recurring manual follow-ups
prioritizes predictable pacing over rapid feature expansion

life_story.childhood_background: Grew up in a household that valued practical decisions and financial caution in a taiwan knowledge work context.
life_story.education_journey: Completed bachelor level education and learned through a mix of formal training and on-the-job repetition during a early career specialist phase.
life_story.career_path: Moved into program coordinator work because it rewards reliability and the ability to keep moving parts aligned. Workflow maturity is currently repeatable, built on steady habits rather than automation.
life_story.family_story: Lives in a multi-generational household of six in Taipei. Shared spaces mean constant low-level interruptions, which reinforces a preference for structured, predictable routines and protects quiet focus windows.
life_story.current_daily_routine: Weekdays split between cross-functional updates, chasing responses across messaging apps, and guarding small blocks of uninterrupted time. Schedule pressure is low, but context-switching drains energy. Decisions move at a committee pace, requiring alignment before action.
recent_life_events: recently reviewed software spend to eliminate redundancies
trying to reduce context-switching during the week by consolidating notifications
evaluated a few task managers but abandoned them after two weeks of friction
frustrations: too many tools pulling attention in different directions
manual follow-up that feels like second job
context scattered across chats, emails, and shared drives
hidden_needs: wants confidence that a new tool will simplify the week quickly
needs a reason to trust the workflow before sharing real work into it
seeks a sandbox environment to test with dummy data first

behavior_profile.buying_behavior: waits until value is concrete enough to justify habit change; prefers gradual rollout over big-bang migration
brand_trust_signals: specific user stories from similar roles
transparent pricing with no hidden tiers
decision_blockers: requirement to train others before seeing personal benefit
emotional_triggers: feeling more in control of follow-up
seeing a direct before-and-after workflow comparison
behavior_profile.tech_adoption_pattern: tests with non-critical tasks first; migrates real work only after peer validation and successful trial period

latent_pain_points: mental load from tracking too many loose ends
slow drift between promises and actual follow-up
constraint_factors: requires mobile-friendly interface due to smartphone-first usage
must integrate with existing messaging habits rather than replace them entirely
needs export capabilities to satisfy internal audit expectations

sensitive_reality_layer.social_risk_profile: does not want to look careless with colleagues' or clients' information; avoids public complaints about vendors
sensitive_reality_layer.fairness_and_inclusion_profile: expects product language to remain respectful and not assume one type of user; notices when onboarding excludes less technical audiences
response_boundaries: will not share private third-party data without a clear reason
flags wording that could exclude less-confident users
prefers neutral, role-agnostic examples over demographic assumptions
sensitive_reality_layer.data_handling_limit: won't input client names or financial figures until privacy controls are verified and documented

audit_evidence_layer.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.
